status.recommended_next_action: Continue detailing the artifact schema packet-by-packet, then define migration unit and quality gate, and only then confirm the draft specs.

reason_summary: Artifact schema, migration-unit boundary, and quality-gate contracts are not yet fixed.
Behavior equivalence and deterministic E2E strategy are still draft and not yet confirmed.
Evidence artifacts and core skill I/O contracts are not yet specified.
Quality gates and characterization oracle requirements are not yet deterministic.
Evaluation program, migration-unit rules, and eval harness ownership are not yet fixed.

decisions.decision: Target is a repo-independent modernization workflow in orchestration/eval/gates, not a single universal transformation prompt.
Beta prioritizes precision over breadth.
Assume the AsIs repository is runnable locally.
Primary beta goal is behavioral equivalence between AsIs and ToBe.
Feature addition and feature improvement are out of scope for beta.
Performance improvement is out of scope unless trivially low-risk.
Security issue remediation should be included in scope.
Use .github/skills/skill-creator as the meta-harness for building modernization skills.
Adopt Fixed cohort + Rolling expansion: start with 1 canonical, 2 near-neighbor, and 1 adversarial repo, then add repos only for new failure families or support-matrix expansion.

Beta deliverable is a single modernization workflow for a fixed FROM/TO scope, composed of multiple core skills, orchestration, intermediate artifacts, and deterministic quality gates; it is not a single giant skill.

Support matrix is fixed to Java legacy server-rendered web (Struts 1 / Servlet + JSP / classic Spring MVC, Java 5-8, Ant or Maven) modernized toward Spring Boot + React using a Strangler Fig strategy with screen/route-level migration units.

Draft proposes judging behavior equivalence per migration unit using observable behavior, with must-match, allowed-difference, and must-not-change rules backed by evidence artifacts and 4-way outcomes: pass/fail/partial/undetermined.
Draft proposes accessibility-first characterization E2E with separate generation/execution and controlled network, time, randomness, data, and session state.

Artifact schema will use a common envelope plus typed packets, with a medium-weight envelope, a standard packet set (ui/http/db/session-auth/external-io/comparison), and unit-dependent must-capture rules prioritized for reproducibility and feasibility.
Artifact schema should mechanically judge everything that can be mechanically judged by comparing canonical fields first; raw evidence is retained primarily for diagnosis and limited human review.

UI flow packets use accessibility-first canonical fields plus diagnostic raw evidence refs; HTTP packets use canonical request/response summaries plus diagnostic raw HTTP refs; DB side-effect packets are required only when declared by unit manifest and compare existing data-contract side effects via change summaries rather than full row snapshots.

Session/auth packets judge authentication and authorization semantics rather than raw cookie or session values, and become required only when the unit manifest declares auth preconditions or protected behavior.

External I/O packets judge integration semantics rather than raw payload equality, use payload meaning summaries plus delivery/failure semantics, and become required only when the unit manifest declares critical external side effects.

Comparison/verdict packets aggregate packet-level comparisons into rule-based unit judgments with 4-state outcomes (pass/fail/partial/undetermined), but beta gates and evaluations treat only pass as success and reject all other outcomes.

Beta keeps the DB schema and business data contract stable by default while allowing schema-nonbreaking access-layer modernization needed for the ToBe runtime; schema migration, bulk data migration, and root-cause DB version remediation are out of scope and should escalate.

todos.title: Define intermediate artifact and evidence schema
Define migration unit and strangler seam model

target_repo_strategy.notes: Start with 4 repositories total as the initial fixed cohort. Add repos only for new failure families, support-matrix expansion, or stronger holdout/regression coverage.
